###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL needs your help.

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-03-13T13:41:39+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aad6cf9_1@news.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<3aa5b838.87188106@news1.attglobal.net> <3aac4bbc_1@news.newsfeeds.com> <3AACDD9D.89353019@brazee.net>`

```

Howard Brazee wrote in message <3AACDD9D.89353019@brazee.net>...
>"Peter E. C. Dashwood" wrote:
>
>> 1. COBOL does not need anybody's help. It is alive and kicking and
probably
>> in better shape now than it has been for its entire life. The Language is
>> fine; the public perception of it is wrong. This perception has arisen
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>Everybody says its perception needs to be changed.  Not many people say
why.  (I
>have some ideas).
>
>Why does it need to be changed?
>At what cost are we willing to change it?  (cost in changing CoBOL from
what it
>is, in making it expensive to make compilers, in upgrading systems, etc.)
>
A very good question Howard.

Notice I am not advocating "change COBOL" (well, assuming we EVER get the
2002 standard...<G>), I am advocating that we change people's perception of
it.

Why? Because the average Joe only knows what he reads in the papers and,
sadly, this also extends to middle Managers who really should know better.
(I'm saying nothing about Senior Management and what they know....<G>)

Jimmy, made the point elsewhere in this thread that SOMEONE has to get to
these Managers (and it ain't gonna be the ANSI people...).

So the reasons Why? are as follows:

1. There is a huge wealth of mis-information about COBOL already in
circulation. This in and of itself wouldn't matter (it is only opinion) but
it colours decisions as to what tools and methodologies should be employed
and COBOL is not even considered, when, on merit, it should be in many
cases.)

Incidentally, the reason for this mis-information is because many COBOL
people have no idea what is available in the latest releases of the language
or how or why they should use it. There is still a "fortress COBOL" attitude
on many installations which strongly resists even LOOKING at the possibility
of changing the way things are done... Reactionary attitudes communicate to
others and before you know it, the language is labelled as a "dinosaur"...

2. COBOL has a VITAL role to play in the emerging technologies and there is
a real danger it will be sidelined because of its "reputation" and NOT
because it is not up to the task. This is a very good reason why we should
strive to get the perception of it changed.

3. What can be done by the average COBOL programmer working in a shop where
COBOL is the "poor relation" and all the sexy stuff is happening in the VB
and Java Groups? My advice is as follows:

1. Learn Java. Two reasons...1: It will teach you the Object Oriented theory
that you need to deal with the new languages, and the "new" COBOL. OO is an
intrinsic part of Java  and it is clear and simple when you study it as part
of Java.
(Unfortunately, the same can not be said for OO COBOL which bogs it down in
a heap of  (maybe necessary) baggage...once you have the concepts, OO COBOL
is easy, but personally, I found out more about OO in two weeks of Java than
I did in three months of OO COBOL...(maybe that's just me; your experience
might be different...anyway, Learn Java.)) Reason 2: You NEED Java as a
COMPLEMENT to COBOL, NOT a replacement for it. Yes, you CAN write CGI  and
ISAPI scripts in COBOL, but it is murder...(take my word; I've done it...)
The same functionality can be provided by a Java Servlet in a fraction of
the time to develop.  Again, "the BEST Tools for the job".

2. Be courteous, polite and helpful to Users. (Even when they ARE stupid and
tiresome...<G>) Promote the image that, as a Professional COBOL programmer
you are there in support of the Business (NOT the technology...). Discuss
the business problems with them, try to understand their needs and meet
them. (One reason COBOL has a "bad press" is because of the historical
arrogant attitude of many COBOL Programmers...this has to change. We must be
 seen as the "friends of the Business".

So, coming back to original question, Howard. Why do we need to change the
perception?

Short answer: Because it is wrong, it sells COBOL and ourselves short, and
if we don't change it, an extremely useful programming language WILL fade
into oblivion.

Over and above all that, COBOL is FUN! That alone is a good enough reason in
my book...<G>

Now, let's hear those ideas of yours that you alluded to...

Pete.




-----= Posted via Newsfeeds.Com, Uncensored Usenet News =-----
http://www.newsfeeds.com - The #1 Newsgroup Service in the World!
-----==  Over 80,000 Newsgroups - 16 Different Servers! =-----
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL needs your help.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-03-13T07:34:47-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AAE3007.4A4FACCC@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<3aa5b838.87188106@news1.attglobal.net> <3aac4bbc_1@news.newsfeeds.com> <3AACDD9D.89353019@brazee.net> <3aad6cf9_1@news.newsfeeds.com>`

```
"Peter E. C. Dashwood" wrote:

> So, coming back to original question, Howard. Why do we need to change the
> perception?
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> Now, let's hear those ideas of yours that you alluded to...

My main idea is that there are significant numbers of management who don't take
the time to learn which tools are appropriate.  They want to be "leading edge"
regardless of what that does to the bottom line.  They need to be able to tell
their peers why they are running a CoBOL shop.

Also, most shops are not set up for internal training.  Training is a different
skill than their core competencies.   So if they cannot send employees to get
trained locally in CoBOL, then they will send them to get trained in less useful
tools.  Managers are aware of this, so they try to guess which training will be
available during the life cycle of their system.  It is to our advantage that
they include CoBOL as a possible tool when making their decision.
```

---

## Thread: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments) — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T00:36:55-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Just because another thread brought up my "public" opinions on this topic,
I thought I would point anyone *interested* to the "detailed" explanation
article that appeared in CobolReport this week.  If you haven't seen it (and
want to look at it), it may be viewed at:

 http://www.cobolreport.com/cobol2002/
```

#### ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@neo.rr.com.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T12:41:27-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<855F31D7CFA4BB92.37044668757C1694.443F38240D0FB451@lp.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
On Wed, 7 Mar 2001 00:36:55 -0600, "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@ix.netcom.com> enlightened us:

>Just because another thread brought up my "public" opinions on this topic,
>I thought I would point anyone *interested* to the "detailed" explanation
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> http://www.cobolreport.com/cobol2002/

In your report you state:

"In general, I would like to ask the relatively simple question: What
difference will it make to you, to any existing COBOL programmer, or
COBOL vendor,  if, on June 24, 2001, there is no ANSI COBOL Standard?
(isn't it possible that it will actually be an improvement as vendors
and users will be forced into talking to each other rather than using
ANSI procedures as an excuse for not  resolving outstanding issues and
questions?) "

Consider this:  most government (and some private) contracts for
software and hardware require that a certain ANSI or ISO standard be
followed.  If there is no ANSI standard for Cobol, might that mean
that new software written for the government would have to be in a
language that is ANSI compliant?  

You stated that this reaffirmation does not effect ISO one way or the
other.  I would beg to differ.  If ANSI is dropped, how long before
ISO is also dropped?  

Concerning users and vendors talking to each other if there is no ANSI
standard:  I really doubt that is going to happen.  I think for those
vendors that want to stay in the compiler writing business, especially
on the PC side, they're going to, without a standard to follow, go off
and develop their own functions and maybe syntax.  I don't see IBM
doing any such thing since they are just about the only player in the
mainframe market anyway, but the PC market is suspect.

You also stated in the report:

"My final question is to anyone who actually believes that it is
critical that the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and amendment or
amendments) be reaffirmed: "

I'm starting to believe that it is critical to have a standard.  The
reasons you give for not affirming:

" does not seem to me to be any existing group (Technical
   Committee or otherwise) with both the resources and the priorities
to actively maintain this Standard - if it were reaffirmed."

makes no sense since the standard has existed for many years in just
the same atmosphere and there seems to be a willingness among vendors
to follow the standard because it is "official".  

Just because J4 can't get there act together shouldn't have a
detrimental effect on whether Cobol is ANSI / ISO certified or not.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

I feel like I'm diagonally parked, in a parallel universe.


Help Save The Rainforest
For more info, please see:  
http://www.ran.org/ran/

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T21:29:19-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<986ubs$poo$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <855F31D7CFA4BB92.37044668757C1694.443F38240D0FB451@lp.airnews.net>`

```
FYI -
  US Government contracts usually require support for the FIPS *not* the
ANSI Standard.  These two are not identical (and haven't been for years).

  For example,
    The Intrinsic Function module is *required* in the HIGH level FIPS
Standard but is optional in the ANSI Standard.

Furthermore, as far as my research indicates *no* current COBOL vendors are
"validating" their current (new) releases of their COBOL compilers.

I do hope that the ISO Standard "falls" soon - but the information that I
have is that this is currently "highly unlikely".  However, once again, I
don't see that making much difference because vendors are NOT implementing
all of the current Standard.

Ask your vendor for support for the Correction Amendment requirement for
continuation after a divide-by-zero - with no ON SIZE ERROR phrase - for
example.  IBM documents that they don't support this.

Unisys documents that they don't handle ":" in Copy/Replacing according to
the current ANSI Standard.

The bottom-line - however, is that if you think the ANSI Standard is a "good
thing" - then I do encourage you to send in your comments BOTH on the
reaffirmation and the current Public Review period.
```

#### ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T15:35:47-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<986gjl$epq$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I have submitted my initial feedback to the article to which Bill Klein
refers at the site on which it appears.  That article opposes the
reaffirmation of the standards for the COBOL language that are currently in
force. I felt it appropriate to post similar comments in this forum as well.

Unfortunately, prior comittments over the next two weeks prevent me from
preparing an in-depth response to Bill's article in a timely fashion, but I
feel I am ethically obligated, not only on behalf of myself but my company
and the customers we serve, to register my disagreement with him on this
matter.   I hope to prepare a more in-depth response to his article within a
few weeks.

I have personally been involved with development and maintenance of several
COBOL compilers over the last sixteen years.  Based on that past *and
current* experience I strongly believe having a standard to which all
implementors are expected to adhere makes for a better and a more consistent
language for all users of that language.  I am strongly opposed to allowing
the current standards to lapse, and I am strongly in favor of the adoption
and refinement of the current Final Committee Draft as a new standard, as
expeditiously as possible.

Moreover, as the primary representative on NCITS/J4 for Unisys Corporation,
I believe I can state on behalf of my management that Unisys supports both
the reaffirmation of the current standard and the continued efforts to
produce its successor, believing that both positions are not only in the
best interests of Unisys but also in the best interest of its current and
future customers.

    -Charles C. Stevens
    -Unisys Corporation
    -NCITS/J4 Primary Representative

news:984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net...

(and
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T21:32:26-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
Chuck -
  If you support the reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard, can
you comment on your company's committtment to implementing the Correction
Amendment specification of how to handle ":" in "partial-word"
COPY/REPLACING statements?

If you think the current Standard SHOULD be reaffirmed but do not actually
implement the explcit specifications in that Standard, can you explain what
you think "reaffirmation" means?
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T12:37:40-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<988qhf$t0c$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <986gjl$epq$1@mail.pl.unisys.com> <986ui3$6gi$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
On 7 March 2001 at 7:21PM, William M. Klein wrote:

<<If you support the reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard, can
you comment on your company's committtment to implementing the Correction
Amendment specification of how to handle ":" in "partial-word"
COPY/REPLACING statements?>>

My company is committed to correcting the MCP/AS COBOL85 bug that prevents
the use of partial words in COPY...REPLACING and REPLACE statements.   At no
point was it our intent that this compiler be noncompliant; the implementors
(and subsequently I) simply misunderstood the implications of the standard
(and more particularly of the differences from the '74 standard) in this
area.    The entire COBOL85 team as well as the field support staff involved
in languages has been made aware of this error in our collective
understanding.

We have been and remain committed to compliance with the standard in this,
and all, areas.   This deficiency in MCP/AS COBOL85 is being treated as a
bug fix and is queued for correction.

We encourage our MCP/AS COBOL85 users to submit trouble reports for any
discrepancies between our implementation and the applicable standard, and
the submission of trouble reports through the field support organization is
the most effective way to get such deficiencies resolved.

<<If you think the current Standard SHOULD be reaffirmed but do not actually
implement the explcit specifications in that Standard, can you explain what
you think "reaffirmation" means?>>

I'm having trouble with the logic flow of this question; it strikes me that
there is at least one presumption in it that's incorrect .  I will thus
address its component phrases:

<<If you think the current Standard SHOULD be reaffirmed>>

Yes, I and my management believe that doing so serves not just Unisys but
more importantly our customers.   As suppliers of compilers that we work
hard to ensure are indeed compliant, we would find the lapse of the current
standards a great loss to us and a disservice to our customers.

<< but do not actually implement the exact specifications in that Standard,
>>

To the best of our ability, and to the best of our understanding,we do
implement the exact specifications of that Standard. There may be lapses in
the first case and mistakes in the second, but both are simply errors and
they are recorded and corrected as such.   No human endeavor is without
flaw.  We correct flaws in our products that we discover and that are
reported to us.

<<can you explain what you think "reaffirmation" means?>>

Since we *do* support reaffirmation of the standard, and we *do* implement
to the standard to the very best of our ability, there is no point in
providing an answer based on the combination of premises raised, because
that combination (introduced by "IF") is false.

Nevertheless, I will state that I believe "reaffirmation" in this context is
a formal recognition that the standard should continue in force.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Reaffirmation of the current ANSI COBOL Standard (and its two amendments)

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T23:38:11-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<989q9i$59u$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<984kv4$7mq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <986gjl$epq$1@mail.pl.unisys.com> <986ui3$6gi$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net> <988qhf$t0c$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`

```
Chuck (and any other vendors watching this thread),

I would SUGGEST that you go thru the "Substantive Change" section in the
Correction Amendment and run the tests that are "listed" there (i.e. by
example).  It is now 8 years since the example for COPY/REPLACING was placed
in an approved amendment.  If you had "tested" it when that Standard was
"out for review" - it would now be a decade since you would have known how
this was REQUIRED to work in an ANSI (or ISO or FIPS) conforming compiler.

Besides the COPY/REPLACING example, I strongly suggest that you test your
compiler for the example on page B-36 - in the entry titled,
  "(8) Size error condition without ON SIZE ERROR phrase (1 NUC)."

Again, like the COPY/REPLACING issue, this is clearly documented as required
by the "base" '85 Standard and was not introduced by any subsequent
amendment - but wasn't identified as a substantive change until 1993.

This is *not* intended to "single out" Unisys - which has indeed indicated
their "renewed" interest in J4 - after they let their prior membership
lapse.  It is, however, intended to point out how little attention is paid
(even today) to those COBOL Standards that *do* get approved by ANSI and/or
ISO.

P.S.  Thane (if you are still watching this thread) - how does Fujitsu do
with this same example?
```

```
I was thinking about this.   I was wondering what the most efficient way is of
doing an insert.  It is your "move table(current-top) to table(current-top +1)"
command which I would want to get working efficiently in CoBOL.   Reference
modification might be the best way.  But I want to make sure that the method
used doesn't just work, but it is supported in the standard, so that it will
still work correctly if I change compilers.

Harold Zbiegien wrote:

> Another option is to build the table in sorted order.  Way back when I used
> to code application programs any time we had to load a table in house
…[42 more quoted lines elided]…
```

### Integrating Fujitsu Cobol with Deplhi

- **From:** "Tony Rabelink" <A.Rabelink@inter.NL.net>
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T21:41:46+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aa69c63$0$11024$4d4ebb8e@newnews.nl.uu.net>`

```
Is it possible to integrate Fujitsu Cobol with Delphi ?
```

#### ↳ Re: Integrating Fujitsu Cobol with Deplhi

- **From:** "sandman" <vyjssm@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T11:16:38+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<989huo$jvj@netnews.hinet.net>`
- **References:** `<3aa69c63$0$11024$4d4ebb8e@newnews.nl.uu.net>`

```
I think it is possible.
I know FJ Powercobol can blend with VB.  If it won't work with Delphi,
rewrite an FJ procedural[traditional] program into an oo cobol program,
then convert this oo program into a COM object, it can be used in VB,
ASP.  I guess it is 'visible'  to Delphi, too.
FJ v6.1 manual has explanation how to convert to a COM.

jason
```

<a id="t-8cade3f52b"></a>
## Thread: Copybooks — 5 msgs

### Copybooks

- **From:** jim_royle_myarse@yahoo.com (Jim Royle)
- **Date:** 2001-03-07T22:15:59+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
Can anyone tell me please how you include copybooks in
a cobol program compiled with the free Fujitsu
compiler. In my program I have coded it as 'COPY
copybook'. But when I compile it complains about the
Library not being set up. I think I've done this in
the compiler options but obviously I've gone wrong
somewhere.

Could anybody help please. I've read the documents
that came on the CD but they completely baffled me.

Thanks.

__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?
Get email at your own domain with Yahoo! Mail. 
http://personal.mail.yahoo.com/
```

#### ↳ Re: Copybooks

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T01:37:56+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010307203756.11923.00000212@nso-fq.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
In article <20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>,
jim_royle_myarse@yahoo.com (Jim Royle) writes:

>
>Can anyone tell me please how you include copybooks in
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Exactly how is your COPY statement coded and how is your
'copybook' file saved on your system?

if      COPY  'COPYBOOK'.
    then copybook should have NO Extension
if      COPY COPYBOOK.
    then copybook Could have No Extension or 
              an Extension of CPY or CBL or COB


At least this is the rule that I use when dealing with testing 
versions of libraries.
```

#### ↳ Re: Copybooks

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T02:59:21+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<dACp6.216$sm4.24776@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
One way is to fully qualify the copy, viz:

COPY 'C:\SOURCE\COPYBOOK\MYFILE.COB'.


"Jim Royle" <jim_royle_myarse@yahoo.com> wrote in message
news:20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com...
> Can anyone tell me please how you include copybooks in
> a cobol program compiled with the free Fujitsu
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Copybooks

- **From:** "sandman" <vyjssm@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T22:37:09+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9897dd$oo8@netnews.hinet.net>`
- **References:** `<20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
I use full file name [includes drvie, directory and file name]
in my cobol programs like:
copy 'c:\fsccpy\my.cpy'.
or
copy "c:\fscdat\my.dat" replacing ==(xxx)== by ==abc==.
Forget the name COPYBOOK, it just a simple text file for saving
some typing work.
jason

Jim Royle <jim_royle_myarse@yahoo.com> wrote in message

> a cobol program compiled with the free Fujitsu
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
```

#### ↳ Re: Copybooks

- **From:** "Barry K. Clarke" <bkclarke@qwest.net>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T19:19:06-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AA84BAA.4AC40297@qwest.net>`
- **References:** `<20010307211557.48651.qmail@web10309.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
Try to look through the docs for the file that holds the
copybooks, search copylib, copybooks.  The compiler is
telling  you that its looking for a file with the copybook,
but can't find it.  Also try using an include statement in
the program.

Barry
```

## Thread: import data from cobol application — 5 msgs

### import data from cobol application

- **From:** "don_chisciotte" <don_chischiotte@tin.it>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T12:15:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<BJKp6.18085$yl2.322177@news1.tin.it>`

```
I must import data from a cobol application, but the
files of database haven't the extension and I don't know
nothing about this cobol application.
What Can I found the type of that database?
```

#### ↳ Re: import data from cobol application

- **From:** Rob Lec <RobLec@cinci-rr.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T13:39:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AA78BB4.E67B5289@cinci-rr.com>`
- **References:** `<BJKp6.18085$yl2.322177@news1.tin.it>`

```
Which COBOL did the files come from?   Each vendor has their own unique
filing system.  There are some tools (such as Data Junction) that
convert data from one platform to another, but generally you must
already know what type of file you have to start with.


don_chisciotte wrote:
> 
> I must import data from a cobol application, but the
> files of database haven't the extension and I don't know
> nothing about this cobol application.
> What Can I found the type of that database?
```

#### ↳ Re: import data from cobol application

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T16:28:23+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Hw7q6.170$g4.64722@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<BJKp6.18085$yl2.322177@news1.tin.it>`

```
Read: http://www.f;exus.com/ebd2asc.html
Download, unzip and read the documentation in file COBDATA.ZIP at
http://www.flexus.com/softwaredownload.html
(that might be "softwaredownloads.html" (plural).
```

#### ↳ Re: import data from cobol application

- **From:** Vadim Maslov <vadik-nws@siber.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T00:34:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AAAC8A0.9F1F1BB9@siber.com>`
- **References:** `<BJKp6.18085$yl2.322177@news1.tin.it>`

```
You can use DataReader tools from Siber Systems.

They allow to read Cobol daat files produced
by popular PC Cobol compilers (MF, FSC, RM, Acu)
and to convert this data to non-Cobol formats
(CSV, DBF, read into Crystal Reports).

Source for Cobol program helps but it is not required.

More info at http://www.siber.com/sct/datafile/

Regards,
Vadim Maslov

don_chisciotte wrote:
> 
> I must import data from a cobol application, but the
> files of database haven't the extension and I don't know
> nothing about this cobol application.
> What Can I found the type of that database?
```

#### ↳ Re: import data from cobol application

- **From:** Franco Violi <fvioli@metodo.net>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T10:40:50+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98fgh5$ral$2@fe2.cs.interbusiness.it>`
- **References:** `<BJKp6.18085$yl2.322177@news1.tin.it>`

```
don_chisciotte wrote:

> I must import data from a cobol application, but the
> files of database haven't the extension and I don't know
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

As far as I know, the probability to have no suffix isam files into a pc 
running cobol is for AcuCobol, Vision file system version 3. Try to find in 
the machine an utility vutil.exe or vutil32.exe. If it's ok, you can unload 
the data in sequential format using 
'vutil32 -unload isam_data_file sequential_data_file'.
I hope this helps.
Bye/Ciao
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: OT.. Songs of the century??????

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-03-13T03:17:06+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aacdad7_3@news.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<MPG.15117222f4b2a3d89896a3@news.naxs.com> <8E34DDE4A4B69FE8.26BE2D4B018BA165.7C7EF6E1CA61137B@lp.airnews.net> <98bhqv$29v$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Good game, Bill!!!

here's my crack at it:

1. See Ya later, Accumulator!

2. It's Multiplication, that's the name of the Game...(Awww no...sorry. That
one has no pun in it...)

3. Until the 12th of Never (that's a very long "Perform")

4. Fade in Roy Orbison...."Crying"...

It was all right
to compile
so I compiled for a while
but then I linked it alone
and the object was blown
and the Library overflowed...

Still, they couldn't tell
I hid my tracks so well
and went out for a beer...

And the Night shift came on
And Production was run
like a balloon made of lead
 But I was home in bed
while they were Pryyyyyying
into the stuff it dumped
Pryyyyyying,
while they were flummoxed, stumped.
and I,
dreamed of girls,
and Ferraris,
and chocolate swirls,
while they were pryyyying
into the code I write
Pryyyyying,
it took them half the night
and I,
 came in next day
and all,
 I had to say
"If you guys re-organised some libraries occasionally you wouldn't be
pulling these all-nighters..."
left them crying, crying, crying, crying, crying...<fade out Roy...>

5. Byte and Cray, zero or one... (think Cole Porter..)

6. Puttin' on the Bits...

7. How much is that number in the Window? (woof woof)
    I'm straining my eyes just to see
    How much is that number in the Window? (woof woof)
    These MicroSoft Fonts aren't for me...

8. They tried to sell us egg foo yong (Fujitsu?)

9.  The Old Focus at Home (Merant?)

Christ, these mushrooms are good....

Pete.
```

```
Michael Mattias wrote in message
<8K9r6.1620$Ke3.190719@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>...
>Peter E. C. Dashwood <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam> wrote in message
>news:3aacdad7_3@news.newsfeeds.com...
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>taxpayer-funded prescription drugs to senior citizens.

Ah, well here in our South Pacific paradise you just take a short walk in
the nearest forest   (or "bush" as we call it, but I know that has
unfortunate overtones to North Americans...).

Actually, the mushrooms I referred to were shiitake. I just happened to be
eating some at the time...<G>

Although I don't qualify as a Senior Citizen (yet) the town I live in is
full of 'em. (I think of them as Teddy Bears, always falling over and having
picnics...).

It is popularly referred to as "God's waiting room" because of the
preponderance of said Teddy Bears.

Tauranga is a seaside resort town with a population of 60,000 which
increases to around 200,000 for about 6 weeks over Xmas (our Summer). Sadly,
I'll be leaving soon to go back to serious work in Europe, although I may
stay on here and work on a book until CW2001 in October. An idyllicly
beautiful place.
>
>I sent your mushroom-aided effort to my Congressman, in hopes it kills the
>idea pemanently. (Puttin on the bits? PUH-LEEZE!).
>
Well, you've given me many a smile by your posts here in the past, Michael,
so I'm glad to return the favour....

Pete.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: OT.. Songs of the century??????

- **From:** "Rufio" <davecawdell@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-10T00:40:34+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6Keq6.11878$Xt3.2017744@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`
- **References:** `<MPG.15117222f4b2a3d89896a3@news.naxs.com> <8E34DDE4A4B69FE8.26BE2D4B018BA165.7C7EF6E1CA61137B@lp.airnews.net> <Cmeq6.1368$UJ5.155850@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`

```
Nah - Rachmaninoff's second is fun, but if you only EVER
own one piano concerto, my vote goes for Grieg's Piano
Concerto in A minor (especially Opus 16). Put it on the stereo,
middle of the day, when no-one is in your house - or any
other house in the street. Crank it up to full volume, and
open the windows, and hit play. Hopefully the piece will
be finished before the police turn up.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: OT.. Songs of the century??????

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T17:01:40-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98budn$b54$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<MPG.15117222f4b2a3d89896a3@news.naxs.com> <8E34DDE4A4B69FE8.26BE2D4B018BA165.7C7EF6E1CA61137B@lp.airnews.net> <Cmeq6.1368$UJ5.155850@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net> <6Keq6.11878$Xt3.2017744@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```
On 9 March 2001 at 4:40PM, Rufio wrote:

<<Nah - Rachmaninoff's second is fun, but if you only EVER own one piano
concerto, my vote goes for Grieg's Piano Concerto in A minor>>

... finished in 1868, a little before the time window here ...

<<(especially Opus 16).>>

As distinct from ... ?    Best I can tell, he only wrote one Piano
Concerto -- in A minor, or any other key for that matter ...

<<Put it on the stereo, middle of the day, when no-one is in your house - or
any other house in the street. Crank it up to full volume, and open the
windows, and hit play. Hopefully the piece will be finished before the
police turn up.>>

Lots better pieces  for that activity than Rach 2. Try the transition into
the last movemement until the end of the piece in the Organ Symphony; the
Prologue to Mefistofele; Sibelius' Second Symphony; just about any of the
Nielsen symphonies; the Janacek Sinfonietta; parts of Bluebeard's Castle;
"Tuba Mirum" from just about any romantic-era Requiem; the big choruses from
Carmina Burana, or better still, Catulli Carmina, ...

    -Chuck Stevens









    -Chuck Stevens
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: OT.. Songs of the century??????

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-10T04:17:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AA9ACE2.A8B9AF8@home.com>`
- **References:** `<MPG.15117222f4b2a3d89896a3@news.naxs.com> <8E34DDE4A4B69FE8.26BE2D4B018BA165.7C7EF6E1CA61137B@lp.airnews.net> <Cmeq6.1368$UJ5.155850@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net> <6Keq6.11878$Xt3.2017744@news1.rdc1.az.home.com> <98budn$b54$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`

```


Chuck Stevens wrote:

> On 9 March 2001 at 4:40PM, Rufio wrote:
>
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
>     -Chuck Stevens

Coo ! Aren't we going a bit posh here - the originator said "Song". Much too
difficult to choose :-

- Grieg's Piano Concerto - fell in love with that at school
- Then seeing a Canadian lass, Yvonne de Carlo, voluptuously play Scheherazade
on the silver screen, dutifully saved my pennies to buy the 78's until I had the
Scheherazade Theme by Rimsky Korsakov
- More tears to the eyes - the Spartacus theme from 'Spartacus' by Katchachurian
- used as theme for BBC TV 'The Onedin Line' - crank your volume on this one and
you'll need to go buy a new stereo.
- 'I Vow to thee my country' theme and others, from the Planet Suite by Gustav
Holst
- Jingoistic I know - but Elgar's Pomp and Circumstance
- Certainly the popular themes of Puccini, particularly those in Madame
Butterfly
- The guy that wants to raise the roof - crank up the volume for Tchaikovsky's
1812!
- Almost impossible not to get out your sombrero and start clicking your heels -
if you listen to Ravel's 'Bolero'.

Hey don't forget the moderns :-

Almost anything by George Gershwin, Jerome Kern, Cole Porter and Richard Rogers
with Lorenz Hart or Oscar Hammerstein II; throw into that mix Lerner and Lowe.
Discount 'one memorable tune per show'  Lloyd-Webber, apart from his 'Phantom of
the Opera'.

- Wanna raise the roof again - before your time - crank up the volume on the
young Rosemary Clooney belting out 'Come Onna my House, My House a Come On" -
makes the cement leave the bricks ! (Like 007, my parents' house looked 'shaken
but not stirred').

- Beautifully melodious - Katerina Valente's first version of 'The Breeze and
I".

- And for sheer perfection - don't forget the Chairman of the Board or
Pavarotti.

Now Bill - how the hell do we get back to COBOL <G>

Jimmy
```

```
On Sat, 10 Mar 2001 04:17:42 GMT, "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
enlightened us:


<<a bunch of stuff I never heard of snipped>>

>- Almost impossible not to get out your sombrero and start clicking your heels -

Bolero...yes..it doesn't start my heels clicking..but it does give me
lubricious  visions of Bo Derek!


>Hey don't forget the moderns :-
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>  

Most of the previous is very boring (at least to me).  Phantom of the
Opera is the exception.

>- Wanna raise the roof again - before your time - crank up the volume on the
>young Rosemary Clooney belting out 'Come Onna my House, My House a Come On" -
>makes the cement leave the bricks ! (Like 007, my parents' house looked 'shaken
>but not stirred').
>

Well if you need a pacemaker to keep your heart beating, I suppose
Rosemary Clooney might bring you some excitement, but ladies like
Billie Holliday, Ella Fitzgerald and Janis Joplin are galaxies better.

>- Beautifully melodious - Katerina Valente's first version of 'The Breeze and
>I".
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>Jimmy

Admittedly I'm not much of a classical music fan, but I do remember an
old 78 rpm record my parents had.  This was pre-stereo days.  Hi-Fi
was all the rage.  This particular recording had a tune called
something like Triumphant March into Aida or something like that.  It
contained a trumpet/coronet intro and solo and burst into full
orchestral mode throughout.  Whenever my parents would leave, I'd put
that record on and turn the Hi-Fi up to 10 and have a good old time.  

Regards,

-oOO--(_)--OOo-

I feel like I'm diagonally parked, in a parallel universe.
```

```
Thank God I don't need a pace-maker - le Beau (la belle) Derek - would necessitate me

Holliday and Joplin - nah. Ella, DEFINITELY. In the song contest thing, durability is

Jimmy
```

```
enlightened us:

>
>

>Jimmy

10 of my favorites?  That's a tough one..to pick 10 out of the
hundreds, maybe thousands of songs I like.  The one thing I can say is
my list would not include any thing recent.  But, if I had to pick 10
all time favorite songs and who performed them..

In no particular order:

1)  Comfortably Numb - Pink Floyd
2)  Let It Be - Beatles
3)  Imagine - John Lennon
4)  Summertime - Janis Joplin
5)  With or Without You - U2
6)  Brown Eyed Handsome Man - Chuck Berry
7)  Nobody Knows You When You're Down and Out - Louis Jordan 
8)  Basin Street Blues - Ella Fitzgerald
9)  I Got It Bad and That Ain't Good - Billie Holliday
10)  Teach Your Children - Crosby, Stills, Nash and Young

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-
```

```
On Mon, 12 Mar 2001 20:04:34 GMT, "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
enlightened us:

>
>
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Actually I became fairly good on piano, but did not learn any of the
classics.  The Sinatra remark was there to show what my mother
stressed.  I hated it (Sinatra, Crosby..all those boring singers from
that time).  From the time I was a baby up until now I've always had a
radio in my room and spent a lot of time listeneing to it.  In my
younger formative years I listened to the "pop" music that was being
played (in the 50's) and on sundays I'd search the dial looking for
gospel because I thought it was cool.  Somewhere along the line I
discovered the Blues and those and R&B (along with hard rock, folk
rock and jazz) have been my staples ever since.

By the way, the piano playing led to trumpet and trombone playing
which led to guitar playing.  Quite a progression all of which ended
with the Viet Nam war.

>Your # 4 below - seems to me that's from one of those 'boring' American
>composers. Along with perhaps another dozen good recordings of same, Janice is
>singing from Gershwin's 'Porgy and Bess' - so there !
>

The song is listed not because of who wrote it, but Janice's rendition
which I believe to be the best I've ever heard.  That song was written
to be a "torch" song but lends itself much more readily to being a
blues tune.

># 8 - Ella - heard her recording of 'Pete Kelly's Blues' from movie of same
>name ? It also starred Jack Webb (Dragnet - Da Dee Dah Dah. Just wanna get the
>facts Ma'am. Just wanna get the facts), Peggy Lee  and the young Janet Leigh -
>yummy !
>

I think I vaguely remember that movie and somewhere in my music
collection I might have an album by the same name.  I'll have to look.
People forget that Jack Webb did a few movies and was also a director
of some note.  I've read that his character on Dragnet, Sgt. Friday,
was really what Jack was like.


>> >>In no particular order:
>> >>
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>> >>10)  Teach Your Children - Crosby, Stills, Nash and Young

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

I like kids.......... But I don't think I could eat a whole one.
```

```

"Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
>
> Lots better pieces  for that activity than Rach 2. Try the
transition into
> the last movemement until the end of the piece in the Organ
Symphony; the
> Prologue to Mefistofele; Sibelius' Second Symphony; just about any
of the
> Nielsen symphonies; the Janacek Sinfonietta; parts of Bluebeard's
Castle;
> "Tuba Mirum" from just about any romantic-era Requiem; the big
choruses from
> Carmina Burana, or better still, Catulli Carmina, ...

Those are mere sighs and whispers compared to Liszt's Les Preludes.

But the best lease-breaker I ever heard was an old Decca recording of
the 1812 Overture performed by the New York Philharmonic augmented by
the U.S. Marine Corps Band, the guns of the U.S.S. Missouri, and the
carillon and bells of the Notre Dame Cathedral. It was so awesome
every copy of the record cracked on the first playing.
```

- **Message-ID:** `<20010308195437.10364.qmail@web10307.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
My COPY statement is coded COPY copybook and my
copybook file is just called 'copybook' with no
extension. (although I have tried it with the
extension of cbl and cpy). I've just tried Jerry's
suggestion of including the full path name and that
seems to work, but I really didn't want to have to do
this. I thought I could just specify the name of the
copybook and in the compiler options somewhere specify
which directory all of my copybooks are kept in. Is
this possible to do ?

__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?
Get email at your own domain with Yahoo! Mail. 
http://personal.mail.yahoo.com/
```

```
uses COBCOPY.

> copybook file is just called 'copybook' with no
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Copybooks

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T00:41:36+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010308194136.18653.00000319@nso-cl.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3AA80214.F3F0B647@dced.state.ak.us>`

```
In article <3AA80214.F3F0B647@dced.state.ak.us>, Calvin Crumrine
<Calvin_Crumrine@dced.state.ak.us> writes:

>
>It might be in an environment variable vs. a compiler option. Microfocus
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Definitely, in FujiCOBOL you can specify the directory to 
search for COPY members.
LIB=<drive>:<copylib path>
As far as I know, the compiler will look in the current directory
AND whatever directory is specified by LIB=
```

<a id="t-a4b3fba853"></a>
## Thread: CONFERENCE: Testing Computer Software (TCS2001) — 1 msgs

### CONFERENCE: Testing Computer Software (TCS2001)

- **From:** paxpaz@bellatlantic.net (USPDI)
- **Date:** 2001-03-08T21:37:11+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.forth
- **Message-ID:** `<paxpaz-0803011636470001@adsl-138-88-75-177.dc.adsl.bellatlantic.net>`

```


   PARTICIPATE IN THE CONFERENCE THAT WILL LET YOU MASTER THE
      NEW CHALLENGES AND DEVELOPMENTS IN SOFTWARE TESTING!

  Mark your calendar for the 18th annual event. The final Conference
program is available on the Web at:

              http://www.uspdi.org/conference

  Brochure requests and downloading the printed brochure can also be done
from this web site.

  The detailed printed brochure is available. You may request the brochure
and other information by e-mail to: admin@uspdi.org
  
++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

         18th International Conference and Exposition on

                   TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE
        Theme: Meeting the New Challenges of Testing

                     June 18-22, 2001
       Washington, D.C. * Hyatt Regency (Bethesda) * USA
   (Conference: June 20-22; Workshops/Tutorials: June 18-19)

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ 

IN COOPERATION WITH: 

Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) SIGSoft
American Society for Quality (ASQ) Software Division
IEEE Reliability Society
Software Technology Support Center (STSC) 

CONFERENCE PROGRAM BY: 
Meredith Consulting

CONFERENCE MANAGEMENT BY: 
U.S. Professional Development Institute

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Message from Conference Chair, Denis Meredith:

             MEETING THE NEW CHALLENGES OF TESTING

Technology for testing and automated solutions has grown remarkably in the
past decade. But the question is still before us -- do we have the support
from technology that we need? Has technology solved the right problems? Is
the technology accessible enough to today's tester? What are the pitfalls
of using such technology? This year, we will be looking at the experiences
with various aspects of testing technology -- some successful, and some
not. 

We look forward to having you share your experiences and learn from
others. I look forward to seeing you there.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

                     ABOUT THE CONFERENCE

SCOPE AND PURPOSE

  The Eighteenth International Conference on TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE
will provide an opportunity to participate in the leading international
conference on software testing. The goal of the 2001 Conference will be to
explore which testing technologies we will need to take into the new
millennium and which ones we will not. Learn how testing professionals and
experts have used advanced methods and testing applications to improve the
effectiveness of their testing, reduce testing costs and meet schedule
deadlines. Share your own experiences in testing with others. You will be
able to exchange knowledge and ideas with people from a wide variety of
environments and locations including those from industry, academia and
government from the United States and abroad. 

  Conference tracks will address topics concerning the management of
software testing, the application of a variety of software testing
techniques, testing case studies, and additional topics that will
introduce more advanced ideas.

  This conference will provide essential information to all who are
involved with or have an interest in software testing. This includes not
only practitioners but managers and researchers as well. 

  The Eighteenth International Conference on Testing Computer Software is
where testing practitioners and managers from the commercial and
applications software community can learn:

* The impact of increasingly complex software on testing
* Ways to improve the testing process
* Practical experience with the various techniques
* Which efforts have been most successful
* Which efforts have been least successful.

  The conference brings together a set of experienced software testers to
share their experiences and what they have learned from them. You will
have the opportunity to hear about effective software testing techniques,
methods and technologies that have benefited the organizations where they
are used.

GENERAL SESSIONS and FEATURED SPEAKERS:

"Real Time Priority and Risk Management for World Class Software Quality"
   by Tom Gilb
"Is That Your Final Answer? Surviving Cross Examination in the Courtroom"
   by Warren S. Reid
"Efficient Testing with a Measure to Select Testing Strategies"
   by John Musa
"Educators Join the Fray -- Tester Training for Undergraduates"
   by Edward L. Jones
"A World-Wide Web of Intrigue -- Deceptive Testing" by Simon Mills
"Wrap-up Session: Conference Reflections" by Marnie Hutcheson

PRE-CONFERENCE WORKSHOPS and TUTORIALS (June 18-19) will present
comprehensive technical sessions for software testing practitioners:

        WORKSHOPS (2 days)
1. "MITS Test Management Method" by Marnie Hutcheson
2. "Fundamentals of Testing Software-Intensive Systems" by Selim Aissi

        TUTORIALS (1 day)
3. "Overview of Testing" by Simon Mills
4. "Test Planning Workshop" by Ross Collard
5. "The Automated Testing Lifecycle Methodology" by Elfriede Dustin
6. "Test Case Administration: Using XML to Exchange Test Case Information
    Between the Business and Test Domains" by Hans Hartmann 
7. "Evolutionary Project Management: How to Get Early Delivery and Project
    Control" by Tom Gilb
8. "More Reliable Software Faster and Cheaper" by John Musa
9. "Software Verification and Validation: An Overview for Practitioners"
    by Steven R. Rakitin
10. "Guided Inspections" by Melissa L. Russ

CONCURRENT SESSIONS will be divided into parallel track sessions,
presenting a variety of management, technical and advanced topics and
issues.

       MANAGEMENT
Implementing a Quality Testing Framework for Iterative
   Software Development in a Regulated Environment
Is There Life After Test?
Managing the Testing of Large Systems
Quality Concepts for Early Testing
Risk-Based Testing
Testing Strategies for a Small Information Technology
   Organization
Certifications for the Software Quality and Test Professional

       TECHNOLOGY
A Message Sequence Chart-Profile for Graphical Test Specification,
   Development and Training
Automatically and Transparently Distributing the Execution of
   Regression Test Suites
Automating Test Case Generation from SDL Specifications
Development of a Benchmark for Engine Control Micro-Controllers
Eliminating Requirement Defects and Automating Test
Testing and Certification in J2EE Development Environments

       AUTOMATION
Applying Simulation Technologies to Manage Test Practices
Coverage Analysis for Model-Based Design Tools
Object-Oriented Techniques for Test Management
Quantitative Evaluation of Automated Domain Testing
Coverage Analysis of Safety-Critical Software
Testing Commercial-Off-The Shelf Components with Software Wrappers

       CASE STUDIES 
Efficient Methods for Testing the Space Telescope Science Planning
   and Scheduling System
Evolution of Testing -- 1998-2001
What Were the Requirements?
Testing Life Cycle of Vendor-Supplied Shrink-Wrap Software:
   A Quality Approach for Managing Modifications
The Operational Testing of Large, Geographically Dispersed
   Computer Systems

       REQUIREMENTS
Evaluating Requirements for Testability
Scenario Analysis as a Tool for Requirements Engineering and Test
   Case Planning
Use Cases, Test Cases, and Function Points -- Where's the Fit?

       PRACTICES
"I Don't Need to Write a Test Plan!"
How to Measure Exploratory Testing

       WEB
Web Testing at the Speed of the Internet 
White Box Testing for Web Applications


VENDOR EXHIBIT of products and services will provide valuable support
services and software information to your computer software testing
efforts.

CONFERENCE VOLUME: A set of conference proceedings, papers and view graphs
will be provided to each participant of the Conference. These materials
are useful not only during the Conference as learning guides, but also as
useful references when the conference is completed.

NETWORKING:

Birds-of-a-Feather Sessions allow you to meet and network with the
speakers and other conference attendees, and discuss areas of mutual
interest.

Lunch Discussion Groups will facilitate the exploration of common interest
topics among the participants.

CONFERENCE LOCATION:

The Testing Computer Software Conference will be held in suburban
Washington, D.C., USA, a major center for government agencies and
educational institutions. The Conference location will be the Hyatt
Regency Hotel in Bethesda, Maryland, easily accessible from interstate
highways or the Metro subway. There is a convenient subway stop beneath
the hotel with elevator access. Washington, D.C. is served by three major
international airports. The Washington area also offers many extraordinary
museums, memorials, parks and other attractions for visitors. 

                            * * * * *

For additional information please contact: 

Regular Mail: USPDI, 612 Ethan Allen Avenue, Suite 100;
              Takoma Park, MD 20912-5400, USA.
Phone:        [+1] 301-270-1033
Fax:          [+1] 301-270-1040 
E-mail:       admin@uspdi.org (Subject: TCS2001)
Web:          http://www.uspdi.org/conference

                                * * * * *
```

<a id="t-114e676a54"></a>
## Thread: RM COBOL, Netware, and file corruption — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### RM COBOL, Netware, and file corruption

- **From:** i_dont_like_spam@baywestpaper.com (Loren Lang)
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T15:18:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aa8ee83.4114368@news.kih.net>`

```
We're using RM COBOL v5.2 for DOS (some 5.3) which worked flawlessly
under Netware 3.12.  Since we upgraded to Netware 4.11 (1 1/2 years or
so) it seems we've been experiencing more file corruption problems.

The BIGGER problem, though, is that the RECOVER utility doesn't work
anymore, so now, instead of the users being down for the time it takes
to recover the file, they are down for the time to restore from backup
as well as losing any work down since then.  This is quite so bad if
it happens early in the morning, but when it happens at 4:15 pm - the
stuff really hits the IS department in the fan.

I know we need to upgrade to a later version of COBOL, we've been
fighting that battle for a couple of years now, but we're a small shop
and are already facing a multi-year backlog of changes, additions and
new features/systems.

Has anyone had or heard of a problem similar to this?  Any ideas are
greatly appreciated.



Loren Lang                      Phone: 859-734-0538 x439
Senior Programmer/Analyst       Fax:   859-734-8210
Bay West Paper Corporation      Replace the obvious with 'llang' to e-mail

-----BEGIN GEEK CODE BLOCK-----
Version: 3.12
GCS/O d- s: a C++$ UL>++ P+ L+>++ E? W++(-) N+ o K- w+ O-@
M? V? PS-- PE++ Y+(++) PGP>+ t+@ 5? X+ R- tv>- b+(++) DI++
D(---) G>++ e++ h---- r+++ y++++
------END GEEK CODE BLOCK------
```

---

```
fighting that battle for a couple of years now, but we're a small shop
and are already facing a multi-year backlog of changes, additions and
new features/systems.
```

```
corruption every weeks, and sometime 2 time a days.  We are going crazy.

error.

recovery on this file.bak and we copy back this file over the old one  (ex:
file.001).  After this process it works find.

We are trying the new Runtime 7, but we have to change our printing
technique since capture command always open a DOSWindows.

Hope this help.
Eric
```

```
>Hi,

…[6 more quoted lines elided]…

You mean like:

READ RECORD AT END
    MOVE 'Y' TO EOF-FLAG.

IF EOF-FLAG NOT EQUAL 'Y'
  ADD 1 TO RECORD-COUNTER.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

I feel like I'm diagonally parked, in a parallel universe.


Help Save The Rainforest
For more info, please see:

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: RecordCounter

- **From:** "Sal Cambareri" <sal@lasermax.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T16:57:48-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<IYXq6.714$mD2.149161@nntp2.onemain.com>`
- **References:** `<3aa93fcb$0$386$446d705c@news.skynet.be>`

```
Acucorp provides with their COBOL runtime, their vision (ISAM) utility
program, vutil.  It has a lot functions, and the -info function will tell
you the record count.

i.e.

vutil -info <yourfilenamehere>

will tell you several basic statistics about your ISAM.

(You may have to hunt for the exact utility program name; sometimes the
utility is named vutil32, but you get the idea.)


"Rudi p." <rudi.paulus@skynet.be> wrote in message
news:3aa93fcb$0$386$446d705c@news.skynet.be...
> Hi,
>
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

<a id="t-ac55105421"></a>
## Thread: REAFFIRMATION of ANSI Standard - Current Tally — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### REAFFIRMATION of ANSI Standard - Current Tally

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-09T15:16:45-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98bh97$f81$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
FYI,
  As of this "posting" the current tally of Public Review comments on the
reaffirmation of the '85 Standard -  that have made it to J4 is
   18 - Yes, reaffirm
     1 - No, don't reaffirm (and this was mine)

Several of the ones asking for "reaffirmation" have asked for ANSI/NCITS
(whoever) to find a way to make an "exception" to their normal rules to
allow the base document and its two amendments all to be reaffirmed - even
though an administrative "glitch" would seem to make this impossible.

I have NO idea if this is/will be possible - but I would add to Thane's
previous request for "input" that those of you who haven't commented yet but
want this Standard reaffirmed *might* want to explicitly ask for this
"exemption" in your Public Review comment.

On the other hand, if there are actually any readers who agree with me that
J4 doesn't have the resources/priorities to "maintain" the current Standard
(and get the next one out as soon as possible), then I would suggest that
you also send in your comments.  Similarly, if you (like me) think that the
current system IS broken and that the only hope for the future of COBOL is
to get it "out of the hands" of ANSI and ISO, (both the current Standard and
the new one), then I suggest that you send that in as well  and NOT assume
that the "administrative snafu" will necessarily do this for you.

As before, the way to submit your comments has been documented in
comp.lang.cobol - or can be found in my article at
    www.cobolreport.com
```

#### ↳ Re: REAFFIRMATION of ANSI Standard - Current Tally

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-03-13T23:05:19+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aadf1c4_2@news.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<98bh97$f81$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```

Pete
```

<a id="t-6890a94b1f"></a>
## Thread: Calling C program from Fujitsu V4 — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Calling C program from Fujitsu V4

- **From:** "Mike Fresenborg" <mike@fresenborg.de>
- **Date:** 2001-03-10T15:48:39
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98det0$s1j$06$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Hi guys,
i want to call a c-program using the fujitsu v4. the call should pass
parameters.
I did several test, e.g  setting compiler option "DLOAD" -> after that i
didn't get compiler/linker error.
But then i got JMP- 0015I-U Cannot call program "FUNCTEST".
Whats wrong ?
I appreciate your help.
.
my c program:
-----------------------
#include <stdio.h>

int main(int argc, char *argv[])
{
  functest(5);
  return 0;
}

functest(int numbers)
 {

 printf("doesn't work, but should !!");
 }


my cobol program:
----------------------
 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. zumtest.
*
 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
 SPECIAL-NAMES.
     DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA.
*
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
*
 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
 01 Anzahl      PIC 9(2) VALUE 5.
*
 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
     CALL "functest" WITH C LINKAGE USING Anzahl.
     STOP RUN.
```

#### ↳ Re: Calling C program from Fujitsu V4

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T00:31:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010310193112.06794.00000374@nso-cp.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<98det0$s1j$06$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
In article <98det0$s1j$06$1@news.t-online.com>, "Mike Fresenborg"
<mike@fresenborg.de> writes:

>
>Hi guys,
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I would suggest putting "functest" into a WS field to ensure a
dynamic link.  Although you must have been able to link using a 
LIB/DEF file from the functest.dll creation
Somewhere I read that there is reference on C calling COBOL 
in the User Guide pg 446.  This might be useful in verifying the
data type relationship and parameter passing methods.
```

#### ↳ Re: Calling C program from Fujitsu V4

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T08:38:39+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AAB398F.FA2D601C@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<98det0$s1j$06$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Mike Fresenborg wrote:
> 
> Hi guys,
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> didn't get compiler/linker error.

The DLOAD tells the compiler to generate code to dynamically link at run
time.  This makes it not link to your static routine.

> But then i got JMP- 0015I-U Cannot call program "FUNCTEST".
> Whats wrong ?

It is looking for 'FUNCTEST.DLL' with your routine in it.

You need to include NOALPHAL to get it to call your lowercase
'functest', or use all uppercase for the C name.

You need to have NODLOAD and link in your routine (without the main()
function) plus whatever libraries your version of the compiler tells
you.  This will probably include LibC.lib and something like
F3BICIMP.LIB.

> .
> my c program:
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>  }

Functest accepts a parameter that is a 32 bit value.  The Cobol below
will probably pass a pointer to a two byte area containing the ascii
value '09'.  You could change this by having:

functest(int * numbers)
..
and
   01  Anzahl        PIC S9(8) COMP-5 VALUE 5.

Or using BY VALUE on the CALL


> my cobol program:
> ----------------------
>  IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
>  PROGRAM-ID. zumtest.

>  WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
>  01 Anzahl      PIC 9(2) VALUE 5.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>      STOP RUN.
```

---

```
Product Name: Word 2.0                                                         

Sell Price:    $299.75     Total Variable Cost:  92.00                         

          B6837949003154          144    43,164                                

          05ofit $50,000          385   115,403                                

          Decline 25%             385   115,403 
----------------------------
THEN IT GETS EVEN WORSE:(
----------------------------                                
                   Nexus Software INC.                                         

               Product Cost Analysis as of 02/20/01                            

               based on Total Fixed Costs:$18,000                              

Product Line: M                                                                

Product Name: Automap 2.0                                                      

Sell Price:     $49.00     Total Variable Cost:  12.00                         

          B6837949003154          486    23,814                                

          05o45590412139        1,837    90,013                                

          4555ine 25%           1,837    90,013   

HERE IS MY CODE:

 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. projone.
***********************************
*PURPOSE: SOFTWARE COST ANALYSIS  *
*AUTHOR: DERRICK PIZUR            *
*WRITTEN: 02/02/01.               *
*REVISED:.                        *
*COMPILER: FUJITSU COBOL 4.0      *
*NOTES:.                          *
***********************************
 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
 SOURCE-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
 OBJECT-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
 
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
	   SELECT SOFTWARE-FILE
		ASSIGN TO "A:\Cis260\SOFT.DAT"
		ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
	   SELECT PRINT-FILE
		ASSIGN TO "A:\Cis260\REPORT.TXT"
		ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 FD SOFTWARE-FILE
	   RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS.
 01 SOFTWARE-RECORD.
    05 SOFT-PROGRAM-INFO.
	    10 SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE         PIC X.
      10 SOFT-PRODUCT-NO           PIC 9(4).
      10 SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME         PIC X(18).
    05 SOFT-VARIABLE-COSTS.
	    10 SOFT-PREP-COSTS.
		15 SOFT-LOADING-PER-DISK PIC 9V99.
    	15 SOFT-NO-DISKS-USED    PIC 99.
      10 SOFT-MANUAL-PRINTING      PIC 99V99.
      10 SOFT-SHIPPING-N-HANDLING  PIC 99V99.
    05 SOFT-SELL-PRICE		 PIC 999V99.
    05 SOFT-FIXED-COST       	 PIC 9(5).
 FD PRINT-FILE.
 01 PRINT-RECORD PIC X(80).
 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
 01 subscripts.
     05 A-SUB   PIC 99  VALUE ZERO.
     05 S-SUB PIC 99  VALUE ZERO.
 01 TITLE-LINE-1.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(19) VALUE SPACES.
	   05 FILLER      PIC X(5)  VALUE "Nexus".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(8)  VALUE "Software".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(4)  VALUE "INC.".
 01 TITLE-LINE-2.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(15) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(13) VALUE "Product Cost ".
     05 FILLER      PIC X(9)  VALUE "Analysis ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "as of "  .
     05 FILLER	  PIC 9(2)  VALUE 02.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(1)  VALUE "/".
     05 FILLER      PIC 9(2)  VALUE 20.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(1)  VALUE "/".
     05 FILLER      PIC 9(2)  VALUE 01.
 01 TITLE-LINE-3.
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(15) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(9)  VALUE "based on ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "Total ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "Fixed ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC x(6)  VALUE "Costs:".
     05 TOT-FIXED-COSTS PIC $ZZ,ZZ9.
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-2.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(21) VALUE "Product Cost Analysis".
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-3.
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(36) VALUE "Summary Report by Product Line as of".
     
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-4.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(7) VALUE "Product".
     05 FILLER     PIC X(2) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(9) VALUE "Breakeven".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(4) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(7) VALUE "Profit:".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(6) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(5) VALUE "Price".
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-5.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(2) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(4) VALUE "Line".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(26) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(8) VALUE "Decline:".
        
 01 ANALYSIS-TABLE.
     05 PRODUCT-INFORMATION OCCURS 3 TIMES.
        10 PROD-UNITS   PIC 9(5).
        10 PROD-REVENUE PIC 9(8).
 01 SUMMARY-TABLE.
      05 SUMMARY-LINE OCCURS 6 TIMES.
        10  PRODUCT-LINE          PIC X .
        10  PRODUCT-INFO                OCCURS 3 TIMES. 
        	    15  LINE-REVENUE          PIC 9(5).

 01 DETAIL-TABLE.
      05  FILLER          PIC X(14) VALUE 'Breakeven'.
      05  FILLER          PIC X(14) VALUE 'Profit $50,000'.
      05  FILLER          PIC X(14) VALUE 'Decline 25%'.
* I READ AHREAD IN THE TEXT TO THE NEXT CHAPTER AND USED
* TEACH YOURSELF COBOL IN 21 DAYS AS REFERENCE FOR THE REDEFINES 
* USEAGE.
 01  FILLER REDEFINES DETAIL-TABLE.
	    05  DETAIL-DESC PIC X(14) OCCURS 3 TIMES.
 
 01 WS-ACCUMULATORS.
     05 WS-VARIABLE-COSTS     PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
     05 WS-PROFIT-PER-SALE    PIC 9(8) VALUE ZERO.
     05 WS-REVENUE	    PIC 9(8) VALUE ZERO.
	   05 WS-UNITS     	    PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
     05  WS-SUM-TOTALS OCCURS 3 TIMES.
          10  WS-TOTAL             PIC 9(6).
     05 WS-DECLINE	    PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
 01 EOF-FLAG 		    PIC X(01) VALUE SPACE.
 01 DETAIL-LINE.
    05  FILLER		    PIC X(10) VALUE SPACES.
    05  DESC-PR	            PIC X(15).
    05  FILLER		    PIC X(5)  VALUE SPACES.
    05  UNITS-PR              PIC ZZZ,ZZ9.
    05  REVENUE-PR            PIC ZZ,ZZZ,ZZ9.
 01  SUMMARY-DETAIL.
      05  SUM-PROD-LINE          PIC X.
      05  SUM-REVENUE OCCURS 3 TIMES.
            10  FILLER                   PIC XXX.
             10  SUM-REV-PR        PIC $$$$,$$9.
 01 DETAIL-LINES.
   05 PRODUCT-DL. 
      10 FILLER	              PIC X(13) VALUE "Product Line:".
      10 FILLER 	              PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
      10 DL-SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE   PIC X     VALUE SPACE.
   05 PROGRAM-NAME-DL.
      10 FILLER		      PIC X(14) VALUE "Product Name: ". 
      10 DL-SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME   PIC X(18) VALUE SPACES.
   05 SELL-PRICE-DL.
      10 FILLER     	      PIC X(12) VALUE "Sell Price: ".
      10 DL-SOFT-SELL-PRICE     PIC $$$,$$9.99.
      10 FILLER		      PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
      10 FILLER                 PIC X(21) VALUE "Total Variable Cost: ". 
      10 DL-SOFT-VARIABLE-COST  PIC ZZ9.99.
 01 PRODUCT-LINE-VALUE.
    05 FILLER 	              PIC X(22)  VALUE "GGames".
    05 FILLER		      PIC X(22)  VALUE "BBusiness Applications".
    05 FILLER		      PIC X(22)  VALUE "UUtility Applications".
    05 FILLER		      PIC X(22)  VALUE "DDrawing/Graphics".
    05 FILLER		      PIC X(22)  VALUE "EEducational".
    05 FILLER		      PIC X(22)  VALUE "MMiscellaneous".
 01 PL-TABLE REDEFINES PRODUCT-LINE-VALUE.
    05 PRODUCTS OCCURS 6 TIMES
	     INDEXED BY PL-INDEX.
		10 PL-CODE  PIC X.
          10 PL-NAME  PIC X(21).  
 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 MAINLINE.
 	   PERFORM 100-INITIALIZATION.
     PERFORM 200-MAIN-PROCESS UNTIL EOF-FLAG = 'Y'. 
     PERFORM 600-PRINT-DETAIL-SUM.
     CLOSE SOFTWARE-FILE.
     CLOSE PRINT-FILE.   	
	   	 STOP RUN.
 100-INITIALIZATION.
* INSERT DATE LOGIC
     OPEN INPUT SOFTWARE-FILE
          OUTPUT PRINT-FILE.

     
     PERFORM VARYING S-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL S-SUB > 6
             MOVE SPACES TO PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB)
             PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
                  UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                       MOVE ZERO TO LINE-REVENUE (A-SUB S-SUB)
             END-PERFORM
     END-PERFORM.


	   PERFORM 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
     READ SOFTWARE-FILE.
     MOVE 0 TO S-SUB. 
     PERFORM 300-LINE-BREAK.
 200-MAIN-PROCESS.
      
      IF SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE NOT = PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB)
			PERFORM 300-LINE-BREAK
	   	END-IF.

	    MOVE 0 TO A-SUB.

      PERFORM 201-VARIABLE-COST-CALC.
      PERFORM 202-BREAKEVEN-CALC.
 	    PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 205-PROFIT-UNITS.
      PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 206-DECLINE-CALC.
      PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 500-PRINT-DETAIL.
      PERFORM 700-READ-NEXT-RECORD.

 201-VARIABLE-COST-CALC.
      
     COMPUTE WS-VARIABLE-COSTS = ((SOFT-LOADING-PER-DISK + 1) * 
             SOFT-NO-DISKS-USED) + SOFT-MANUAL-PRINTING +
		   SOFT-SHIPPING-N-HANDLING
     END-COMPUTE.

 202-BREAKEVEN-CALC.
     COMPUTE  WS-UNITS ROUNDED = SOFT-FIXED-COST / 
			(SOFT-SELL-PRICE - WS-VARIABLE-COSTS)
     END-COMPUTE.
     
 203-REVENUE-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-REVENUE = (WS-UNITS * 
             SOFT-SELL-PRICE)
     END-COMPUTE.
     
 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.
     ADD 1 TO A-SUB.
     MOVE WS-UNITS TO PROD-UNITS (A-SUB).
     MOVE WS-REVENUE TO PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB).
     MOVE WS-DECLINE TO WS-DECLINE.
	   ADD WS-REVENUE TO LINE-REVENUE (S-SUB A-SUB).
     ADD WS-REVENUE TO WS-TOTAL (A-SUB).
 205-PROFIT-UNITS.
     COMPUTE WS-UNITS = (50000 + SOFT-FIXED-COST) /
	     (SOFT-SELL-PRICE - WS-VARIABLE-COSTS)
	   END-COMPUTE.

 206-DECLINE-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-DECLINE = (SOFT-SELL-PRICE * .75)
	   END-COMPUTE.

 300-LINE-BREAK.
     ADD 1 TO S-SUB.
*     MOVE SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE TO (S-SUB).

 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
	   PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                 MOVE 0 TO WS-TOTAL (A-SUB)        
     END-PERFORM.
 500-PRINT-DETAIL.
     MOVE SOFT-FIXED-COST  TO TOT-FIXED-COSTS.
     MOVE SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE TO DL-SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE.
     MOVE SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME TO DL-SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME.
*     MOVE WS-UNITS TO 
     MOVE SOFT-SELL-PRICE   TO DL-SOFT-SELL-PRICE.
     MOVE WS-VARIABLE-COSTS TO DL-SOFT-VARIABLE-COST.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-1 AFTER PAGE.
	   WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-2 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-3 AFTER 1.
*     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TOT-FIXED-COSTS AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM PRODUCT-DL AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM PROGRAM-NAME-DL AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM SELL-PRICE-DL AFTER 1.
     
     PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
		UNTIL A-SUB > 3
		MOVE  DETAIL-DESC (A-SUB)  TO DESC-PR
		MOVE  PROD-UNITS (A-SUB)   TO UNITS-PR
		MOVE  PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB) TO REVENUE-PR
		WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DETAIL-LINE AFTER 1
     END-PERFORM.
     PERFORM 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
 600-PRINT-DETAIL-SUM.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-1     AFTER PAGE.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-2 AFTER 1. 
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-3 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-4 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-5 AFTER 1.
      PERFORM VARYING S-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL S-SUB > 6
         MOVE PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB) TO SUM-PROD-LINE         
           PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
             UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                MOVE LINE-REVENUE (S-SUB  A-SUB) TO SUM-REV-PR (S-SUB)   
           END-PERFORM
       WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM SUMMARY-DETAIL AFTER 1 
      END-PERFORM.

     MOVE 'TOTAL' TO SUM-PROD-LINE   
     PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
      UNTIL A-SUB > 3
         MOVE WS-TOTAL (A-SUB) TO SUM-REV-PR (A-SUB)
     END-PERFORM.
 700-READ-NEXT-RECORD.
     READ SOFTWARE-FILE
       AT END MOVE 'Y' TO EOF-FLAG
     END-READ.
*********JOKE*********

* what do u call a cow with no legs?
*
*
*-Ground Beef
*What are four hundred rabbits hopping backwards?
*-A receding hare line
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** Juergen Woischwill <Woischwill@addcom.de>
- **Date:** 2001-03-10T18:00:36+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010310.18003606@mis.configured.host>`
- **References:** `<20010310115657.01919.00000527@ng-cm1.aol.com>`

```

Your Problem is caused by a table Overflow. I think you should check the 
IF-Statement at the beginning of 200-MAIN-PROCESS. This Statement 
increments (via 300-LINE-BREAK) the index S-SUB, without checking the 
maximum value of 6 that S-SUB may have as a valid Index for the 
SUMMARY-TABLE. Thus, depending on your specific Input File, there will be 
an Overflow of SUMMARY-TABLE, which destroys the DETAIL-TABLE.
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T01:14:58-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98f8na$g4h$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<20010310115657.01919.00000527@ng-cm1.aol.com>`

```
See the following code:

>  01 PRODUCT-LINE-VALUE.
>     05 FILLER               PIC X(22)  VALUE "GGames".
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>           10 PL-NAME  PIC X(21).

Each table entry is 22 bytes in the original "table" - but only 21-bytes in
the redefined.  This is going to cause real problems as you "go along"
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T16:30:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010311113057.24849.00000312@nso-bg.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<98f8na$g4h$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
In article <98f8na$g4h$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>, "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@ix.netcom.com> writes:

>
>See the following code:
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I am a bit surprised to see this.
It looks to me that each table entry in the redefine is 
comprised of a total of 22 bytes.  There is defined a
1-byte code and a 21-byte name for each entry.
What problem is this supposed to be causing as you 
"go along" ?
Sorry , but I don't 'see' it.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T11:55:56-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98ge8o$5gj$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<98f8na$g4h$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net> <20010311113057.24849.00000312@nso-bg.aol.com>`

```
You're right and I was wrong.  I missed the 1 byte PL-CODE field
```

<a id="t-ed39794da8"></a>
## Thread: subprograms help me with parameter? — 13 msgs

### subprograms help me with parameter?

- **From:** "MadG" <patrick4133@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T00:17:30+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uuzq6.9371$p66.3321671@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
I am passing a parameter into the attatched code.
I perform the calculation and now I want to return the value.
I'm not sure how to go about it...
I need to place it in the detail line of the calling program.
I would also like to know if it's the same for multiple
parametere,( I assume it is ).

Thanks a bunch!

Patrick,


 DATA DIVISION.

 LINKAGE-SECTION.
 01 LS-ANNUAL-SALARY PIC 9(6)V99.

 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

 01 JOB-VARIABLES.
 05 WS-ANNUAL-SALARY PIC 9(6)V99.
 05 WS-REG-PAY  PIC 9(7)V99.
 05 BI-MONTH  PIC 99 VALUE 26.

 PROCEDURE DIVISION.

 000-PRODUCE-REG-PAY-SALARY.
  PERFORM 100-INITIALIZE-JOB-VARIABLES
  PERFORM 110-CALCULATE-REG-PAY.

 100-INITIALIZE-JOB-VARIABLES.
  MOVE LS-ANNUAL-SALARY TO WS-ANNUAL-SALARY
  MOVE 0 WS-REG-PAY.

 110-CALCULATE-REG-PAY.
  DIVIDE WS-ANNUAL-SALARY BY BI-MONTH
  GIVING WS-REG-PAY
  EXIT PROGRAM.
```

#### ↳ Re: subprograms help me with parameter?

- **From:** "Anthony Borla" <ajborla@bigpond.com>
- **Date:** 2001-03-11T13:25:34+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<McBq6.12278$v5.52935@newsfeeds.bigpond.com>`
- **References:** `<uuzq6.9371$p66.3321671@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
"MadG" <patrick4133@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:uuzq6.9371$p66.3321671@news3.rdc1.on.home.com...
> I am passing a parameter into the attatched code.
> I perform the calculation and now I want to return the value.
…[36 more quoted lines elided]…
>

COBOL sub-program parameters are, by default, *reference* parameters (you
may have come across the 'BY REFERENCE' and 'BY VALUE' clauses of the 'CALL'
verb) which means that the *address* of the linkage item is passed to the
sub-program. This allows two-way communication:

* The caller may place data values into the linkage area

* The callee may, also, place values into the linkage area. On
   return to the caller, the linkage area may be read and any
   value altered by the callee available

Thus, you might want to make a couple of small changes to your code:

* Alter your linkage section:

 LINKAGE-SECTION.
 01 LS-PAY-DATA.
    05 LS-ANNUAL-SALARY PIC 9(6)V99.
    05 WS-REG-PAY  PIC 9(7)V99.

* Alter your 'call' statement in the 'caller':

  CALL "SUBPROG" USING LS-PAY-DATA .....
  ....

* Alter your sub-program:

  110-CALCULATE-REG-PAY.
   DIVIDE WS-ANNUAL-SALARY BY BI-MONTH
   GIVING WS-REG-PAY

   MOVE WS-REG-PAY TO LS-REG-PAY

   EXIT PROGRAM.

   or, you could place the value straight into the linkage area:

   ...
   GIVING LS-REG-PAY
   ...

To answer your other question, yes, you can also use multiple parameters, if
you wish, or just use a single grouped item (each of its elements would be a
particular parameter value).

A couple of other things:

* You could use a parameter to return a 'status' code indicating
  successful execution or not

* You might want to check for 'division by zero' and an 'on exception'
   check in your sub-program

I hope this helps.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: subprograms help me with parameter?

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```

So I assume that :

   I make a linkage section in BOTH
   programs ( I didn't think it was necessary)

   I accept or move the new calculated data directly
   into the CALLING program (in it's linkage or
   working storage?  Does it matter?),
   so the MOVE WS-REG-PAY TO LS-REG-PAY
    ... the LS-REG-PAY is defined in the calling program?


Thanks again,
Pat ;)








Anthony Borla <ajborla@bigpond.com> wrote in message
news:McBq6.12278$v5.52935@newsfeeds.bigpond.com...
```
